title: Selective Transfer Hydrogenation of Furfural to Isopropyl Levulinate: An In Situ One-Pot Cascade Approach
domain: chemistry / homogeneous catalysis / transfer hydrogenation / biomass valorization

claims.text: Table 1: for each catalyst entry the reported product yields (FA, FE, IPL, AL, GVL) are individually <= the reported conversion (no product yield exceeds conversion).
claims.quote: Ru-MACHO entry: Conversion 71; FA 1, FE 14, IPL 30, AL 3, GVL -
claims.section: Table 1 (Transfer hydrogenation of furfural to small oxygenates using Ru complexes and MSA as Bronsted acid)
claims.predicate: For every row, check each product yield <= conversion, and check the sum of detected product yields <= conversion (selectivity bookkeeping).

evidence.quote: 1 Ru-5 conv26 FE1 IPL3 ; 2 Ru-6 conv45 FE1 IPL4 ; 3 Ru-7 conv34 FE2 IPL6 ; 4 Ru-8 conv52 FE6 IPL15 AL1 ; 5 Ru-MACHO conv71 FA1 FE14 IPL30 AL3 ; 6 Ru-MACHO-BH conv99 FE9 IPL50 AL3 GVL15 ; 7 Ru-MACHO-BH(no acid) conv98 FA98

verification.verifier_needed: Per-row yield<=conversion and sum(yields)<=conversion arithmetic check.
verification.rationale: Every individual yield is well below its conversion and the summed detected yields never exceed conversion (e.g. entry6 50+9+3+15=77 < 99; the unaccounted ~22% is plausibly humins/unidentified). Standard yield/selectivity-vs-conversion bookkeeping check. Off-the-shelf yield-consistency verifier applies. Confidence <1 because unidentified products mean we cannot mass-close, only test the inequality.

claims.text: The abstract states the harmonized Ru-MACHO-BH/Bronsted-acid system gives 'a highly selective reaction toward IPL (complete conversion, 67% yield)'.
claims.quote: the harmonization of these two functions leads to a highly selective reaction toward IPL (complete conversion, 67% yield)
claims.predicate: Bind the abstract's '67% yield' IPL claim to a specific table/figure entry. The best IPL yield printed in Table 1 is 50% (entry 6) and in Table 2 is 61% (entry 12, GC) / 53% (isolated). 67% appears in no main-text table; check whether it is supported by SI Table S3/S4 or the Figure 5 overall plot peak.

evidence.quote: complete conversion, 67% yield
Table1 e6 IPL 50 ; Table2 e4 (MSA) IPL 59 ; Table2 e12 (6mmol) IPL 61 (53 isolated)

verification.verifier_needed: Prose(abstract)-vs-table/figure binding: locate the data point that yields 67% IPL; if none exists, flag abstract overstatement.
verification.rationale: No main-text table reports 67% IPL yield (max printed is 61% GC in Table 2 entry 12 / 59% MSA / 50% Table 1). 67% may be a peak value from the Figure 5 overall-plot time-course or SI Table S3 not transcribed numerically here, or a rounding/optimum quoted from the SI. Cannot bind to evidence from the main text, so inconclusive pending SI Table S3/S4 and Figure 5 digitization; the gap (67 vs best-printed 61) is worth confirming, not yet a confirmed contradiction.

claims.quote: the reaction cascade can be directed until the final product, gamma-valerolactone (GVL) (15% yield)
claims.predicate: Check the abstract's 15% GVL yield against the table value. Table 1 entry 6 (Ru-MACHO-BH) reports GVL 15%.

evidence.quote: gamma-valerolactone (GVL) (15% yield)
Ru-MACHO-BH: GVL 15

verification.verifier_needed: Prose-vs-table exact-match of the 15% GVL figure.
verification.rationale: Abstract 15% GVL matches Table 1 entry 6 GVL=15 exactly. Clean prose-vs-table binding, off-the-shelf check. Confidence <1 only because GVL also appears at 15% in Table 2 entry 3 (MSA, 130C) so the binding is consistent across both tables.

claims.text: The higher-MW side product peak at 236.27 g/mol is identified as the mixed acetal 2-((furan-2-yl(isopropoxy)methoxy)methyl)furan.
claims.quote: two main peaks representing the molecular weights of 236.27 and 178.18 g mol-1 (Figures S14 and S15). The higher molecular weight component was identified as a mixed acetal, the 2-((furan-2-yl(isopropoxy)methoxy)methyl)furan
claims.predicate: Recompute the average molecular weight of the named structure from its molecular formula and compare to 236.27. The IUPAC name parses to C13H16O4.

evidence.quote: molecular weights of 236.27 ... identified as a mixed acetal, the 2-((furan-2-yl(isopropoxy)methoxy)methyl)furan

verification.verifier_needed: Molecular-formula -> average molecular weight recompute (T1 exact-mass/atomic-weight lookup) vs reported value.
verification.rationale: Parsed C13H16O4 (furan-2-yl-CH(OiPr)(OCH2-furan): C 4+1+3+1+4=13, H 3+1+7+2+3=16, O 1+1+1+1=4). Average MW = 13*12.011 + 16*1.008 + 4*15.999 = 236.27, matching the reported 236.27 exactly. Clean atomic-weight recompute. Confidence <1 only because the formula derivation depends on correctly parsing the IUPAC name (verified by hand).

claims.text: The lower-MW side product peak at 178.18 g/mol is identified as the ether 2,2'-(oxybis(methylene))difuran (difurfuryl ether), formed from two molecules of furfuryl alcohol.
claims.quote: The smaller molecular weight component was recognized as an ether, derived from two molecules of FA, the 2,2'-(oxybis(methylene))difuran (Figure 6, IntB)
claims.predicate: Recompute the average MW of 2,2'-(oxybis(methylene))difuran = C10H10O3 (= 2x furfuryl alcohol C5H6O2 minus H2O) and compare to 178.18.

evidence.quote: 178.18 g mol-1 ... an ether, derived from two molecules of FA, the 2,2'-(oxybis(methylene))difuran

verification.verifier_needed: Molecular-formula -> average MW recompute vs reported value.
verification.rationale: Difurfuryl ether = 2 x furfuryl alcohol (C5H6O2) - H2O = C10H10O3. Average MW = 10*12.011 + 10*1.008 + 3*15.999 = 178.19, matching reported 178.18 within rounding (0.01). Clean atomic-weight recompute; condensation stoichiometry (loss of one water from two C5H6O2) also checks out.

claims.text: DFT predicts mixed-acetal IntA formation is endergonic, with delta-r G* = 26.1 kJ/mol at 298 K and 46.1 kJ/mol at 403 K.
claims.quote: Model reaction 1 ... IntA ... delta_rG* (298 K) = 26.1 kJ mol-1 ; delta_rG* (403 K) = 46.1 kJ mol-1
claims.predicate: Check internal consistency: text states IntA formation is endergonic (non-spontaneous); both DFT values are positive (26.1, 46.1 kJ/mol), consistent with 'endergonic'. Sign/label consistency between Figure 6 numbers, the prose, and SI Table S5.

evidence.quote: delta_rG* (298 K) = 26.1 kJ mol-1 ; delta_rG* (403 K) = 46.1 kJ mol-1
formation of intermediate IntA at lower temperature (298 K) and at the reaction temperature (403 K) is endergonic, indicating the non-spontaneous nature of the reaction

verification.verifier_needed: Sign-vs-claim consistency (positive dG <-> 'endergonic/non-spontaneous') and prose-vs-figure agreement; cross-check vs SI Table S5 electronic energies + thermal corrections.
verification.rationale: Both reported delta-G values are positive (26.1, 46.1 kJ/mol), correctly matching the prose 'endergonic / non-spontaneous'. Label/sign self-consistent. Cannot independently recompute the DFT free energy from the main text (requires SI Table S5 electronic energies + thermal corrections at DLPNO-CCSD(T)/PBE0-D3 level); a T6/recompute check would need SI numbers. Confidence reflects that only the sign/label consistency is verifiable here, not the DFT value itself.

claims.text: DFT predicts difurfuryl-ether IntB (dimerization) formation is exergonic, with delta-r G* = -14.9 kJ/mol at 298 K and -11.9 kJ/mol at 403 K.
claims.quote: Model reaction 2 ... IntB ... delta_rG* (298 K) = -14.9 kJ mol-1 ; delta_rG* (403 K) = -11.9 kJ mol-1
claims.predicate: Check that the negative DFT values for IntB are consistent with the prose describing dimerization as thermodynamically favorable (the text says IntB forms readily / dimerization is favored over mixed-acetal formation).

evidence.quote: delta_rG* (298 K) = -14.9 kJ mol-1 ; delta_rG* (403 K) = -11.9 kJ mol-1
the formation of IntB is energetically more favorable... the formation of IntB should proceed readily under the reaction conditions. Both reactions are acid-catalyzed

verification.verifier_needed: Sign-vs-claim consistency (negative dG <-> exergonic/favorable) and IntB-more-favorable-than-IntA comparison (-14.9 < +26.1).
verification.rationale: IntB dG values are negative (-14.9, -11.9), consistent with the prose that dimerization is thermodynamically favorable and that IntB formation is more favorable than IntA (negative vs IntA positive 26.1/46.1). Internal sign/comparison consistency holds. The underlying DFT numbers themselves are not recomputable without SI Table S5.

claims.text: For the mixed acetal IntA, the free energy of formation becomes more endergonic on heating (46.1 kJ/mol at 403 K > 26.1 kJ/mol at 298 K), consistent with the stated need for constant water removal.
claims.quote: is endergonic, indicating the non-spontaneous nature of the reaction and the need of constant... [water removal]
claims.predicate: Check monotonic direction: delta-G(403K) > delta-G(298K) for IntA (acetal formation releasing water becomes less favorable / more positive with T), and the opposite mild trend for IntB. Verify the prose's temperature reasoning matches the two numbers.

verification.verifier_needed: Trend/sign consistency of the two-temperature DFT free energies vs the prose's temperature argument.
verification.rationale: IntA becomes more positive with T (26.1 -> 46.1, +20.0), matching the claim that acetal formation is increasingly disfavored at reaction temperature (driving the need to remove water). IntB stays negative and shifts only +3.0. Directionally consistent with the narrative. Lower confidence because the exact T-dependence depends on entropy terms in SI Table S5 not independently recomputed.

claims.text: On 6 mmol scale, IPL was obtained in 61% GC yield, and after column-chromatography purification was isolated in 53% yield (Table 2, entry 12).
claims.quote: when conducted on a 6 mmol scale, 61% of IPL were formed. After purification by simple column chromatography, IPL was isolated in 53% yield (Table 2, entry 12)
claims.predicate: Bind the prose 61% GC / 53% isolated to Table 2 entry 12, which prints IPL '61 (53)'. Check prose-vs-table match and that isolated (53) <= GC (61).

verification.verifier_needed: Prose-vs-table exact match (61, 53) and sanity isolated<=GC.
verification.rationale: Table 2 entry 12 prints IPL '61 (53)', matching the prose 61% GC and 53% isolated exactly; isolated (53) < GC (61) as physically expected (purification losses). Clean prose-vs-table binding plus a sanity inequality. Footnote g confirms '53 represents isolated yield of IPL' for the 6 mmol experiment.

claims.text: Catalyst poisoning experiments (excess Hg(0) or 2 mol% PPh3 added to 0.5 mol% Ru-MACHO-BH / 1 mol% MSA / 2 mmol FF) caused no change in catalytic activity, with IPL afforded in 65% yield in both cases, demonstrating homogeneous catalysis.
claims.quote: Addition of an excess of Hg(0) or the addition of PPh3 (2 mol%), respectively... revealed no changes of the catalytic activity observed previously. In both cases IPL was afforded in 65% yield. This observation conclusively demonstrates a homogenously catalyzed transformation
claims.predicate: Assess method appropriateness: Hg(0) drop test and phosphine poisoning are standard homogeneous-vs-heterogeneous discriminators; check that 'no activity change' is the correct interpretation (poisons suppress heterogeneous/nanoparticle catalysis). Also a mild T2 consistency: 65% poisoned-yield vs the ~61% baseline 6mmol / 59% MSA values.

evidence.quote: In both cases IPL was afforded in 65% yield
MSA conv98 IPL 59

verification.verifier_needed: Method-appropriateness check (Hg drop test + phosphine poisoning correctly used as homogeneity diagnostics) + advisory note on the experimental logic.
verification.rationale: Hg(0) and excess-phosphine poisoning are accepted (if individually non-definitive) tests for homogeneous vs colloidal/heterogeneous catalysis; unchanged activity is consistent with a homogeneous mechanism. The 65% poisoned IPL yield is slightly higher than the 59% baseline (Table 2 e4) and the 61% 6mmol value, so 'no change' is qualitative. This is a T3 design-appropriateness judgment (not a deterministic recompute); the word 'conclusively' is a mild over-statement since single poisoning tests are suggestive, not definitive. Routed to advisory/human for the strength-of-claim nuance.

claims.text: Decreasing the MSA acid concentration from higher loading to 0.5 mol% suppresses IPL formation, giving 59% yield of IPL.
claims.quote: Decreasing the acid concentration of MSA to 0.5 mol% suppresses the formation of IPL with 59% yield in IPL
claims.predicate: Internal logic check: the prose says decreasing acid 'suppresses' IPL but then states 59% IPL yield (the highest IPL among the MSA entries in Table 2). Check whether 'suppresses' is consistent with the reported 59% being a high value (potential wording/logic inconsistency) and bind 59 to Table 2 entry 4 (MSA, 1 mol% per footnote vs 0.5 mol%).

evidence.quote: 3 MSA(1.5mol%) IPL 50 GVL 15 ; 4 MSA IPL 59 AL5 GVL10

verification.verifier_needed: Prose-vs-table binding of the 59% value and internal-logic check on the word 'suppresses' vs a 59% (high) IPL yield.
verification.rationale: 59% IPL is the maximum IPL yield in Table 2 (entry 4), so calling 0.5 mol% MSA a condition that 'suppresses the formation of IPL' reads as awkward/possibly mis-worded (it appears to be the BEST IPL condition, not a suppressed one). The footnotes map MSA loadings ambiguously (entry3 1.5 mol%, entry4 unannotated, entry10/11 0.5/1 mol%), so the exact loading that gives 59% cannot be cleanly bound. Likely a phrasing issue ('suppresses humins/side-products' rather than IPL) but cannot resolve without disambiguating which MSA loading entry equals 0.5 mol%. Inconclusive.

claims.text: At 150 C, full conversion of FF was achieved but the IPL yield dropped to 45% (relative to the 130 C optimum).

claims.predicate: Check the comparative claim that 45% (150 C) is a 'considerable drop' vs the stated 130 C optimum. Needs the 130 C reference IPL value to evaluate the magnitude. If the 130C optimum is ~50-61%, a drop to 45% is modest-to-moderate, so 'considerably' may be an over-statement.

evidence.quote: the yield of IPL (45%) dropped considerably

verification.verifier_needed: Percent-change/magnitude check of 45% vs the 130 C reference IPL value to test whether 'considerably' is warranted.
verification.rationale: 45% at 150 C is reported as a 'considerable' drop, but the 130 C reference IPL value to compare against is ambiguous (50% Table 1, 59% Table 2 MSA, 61% 6mmol). Against 50% the drop is 5 points (~10% rel, not 'considerable'); against 61% it is 16 points (~26% rel). The qualitative descriptor 'considerably' cannot be confirmed/refuted without fixing the baseline. Inconclusive; a percent-change verifier could settle it once the baseline is bound.

claims.text: Table 2 entry 13 reports a control (FFE/footnote) with 7% AC yield and entry 14 reports 10% (FE) at low conversion, included as comparison/control runs.

claims.text: Reaction conditions are internally consistent: 'net acid loading of 1 mol% of MSA' is stated to correspond to 1.5 mol% MSA in the chloride-complex pre-activation case (0.5 mol% KOH base consumed).
claims.quote: in case of the chloride complexes 0.5 mol% of the base were used and 1.5 mol% of MSA (relating to a net acid loading of 1 mol%)
claims.predicate: Arithmetic: 1.5 mol% MSA - 0.5 mol% KOH (neutralized 1:1) = 1.0 mol% net acid. Check 1.5 - 0.5 = 1.0.

evidence.quote: 1.5 mol% of MSA (relating to a net acid loading of 1 mol%) ... 0.006 M/1.5 mol% (MSA), 0.002 M/0.5mol% KOH

verification.verifier_needed: Acid/base stoichiometry subtraction (MSA mol% - KOH mol% = net acid mol%).
verification.rationale: MSA (monoprotic, 1.5 mol%) neutralized by KOH (monobasic, 0.5 mol%) 1:1 leaves 1.5 - 0.5 = 1.0 mol% net acid, exactly as stated. Also consistent with the Table 1 footnote concentrations (0.006 M MSA / 0.002 M KOH -> 0.004 M = 1 mol% net at the stated substrate concentration). Clean T0 stoichiometry/arithmetic check.

claims.text: Table 1 footnote concentration coherence: 0.006 M MSA at 1.5 mol% and 0.002 M KOH at 0.5 mol% imply a substrate molarity of 0.4 M (2 mmol in 5 mL).
claims.quote: furfural 2 mmol, 5 mL IPA, 0.5 mol% cat. 0.006 M/1.5 mol% (MSA), 0.002 M/0.5mol% KOH
claims.predicate: Check unit/concentration coherence: substrate = 2 mmol / 5 mL = 0.4 M. MSA 1.5 mol% of 0.4 M = 0.006 M (matches). KOH 0.5 mol% of 0.4 M = 0.002 M (matches).

evidence.quote: furfural 2 mmol, 5 mL IPA ... 0.006 M/1.5 mol% (MSA), 0.002 M/0.5mol% KOH

verification.verifier_needed: Concentration/mol% consistency: substrate molarity x mol% = molarity for both MSA and KOH.
verification.rationale: Substrate 2 mmol / 5 mL = 0.4 M. 1.5% of 0.4 M = 0.006 M MSA (exact). 0.5% of 0.4 M = 0.002 M KOH (exact). Both footnote molarities are internally consistent with the stated mol% and substrate loading. Clean T0 unit/concentration recompute.

claims.text: The catalysis remains homogeneous throughout the entire reaction coordinate (no oligomer/polymer / humin / high-MW products detected; only two low-MW side products at 236.27 and 178.18 g/mol).
claims.quote: Based on this investigation we could exclude the presence of oligomer/polymer like materials, and we could clearly identify two main peaks representing the molecular weights of 236.27 and 178.18 g mol-1
claims.predicate: Claim<->evidence support: the 'no humins / homogeneous throughout' claim rests on MS detecting only the two named low-MW species (Figures S14/S15). Check that this evidence (absence of high-MW peaks) actually supports 'excluded oligomers/polymers', or whether it is an absence-of-evidence over-claim.

evidence.quote: we could exclude the presence of oligomer/polymer like materials ... two main peaks ... 236.27 and 178.18 g mol-1

verification.verifier_needed: Claim-strength/scope check: does 'no high-MW peaks detected by MS' justify 'excluded oligomers/polymers'? (absence-of-evidence vs evidence-of-absence; depends on MS mass range and detection limit).
verification.rationale: Excluding oligomers from a single MS that shows two low-MW peaks is an absence-of-evidence argument; validity depends on the MS scan range and ionization efficiency for any humin/polymer (in SI Figures S14/S15, not numerically transcribed). The lower-than-100% mass balance in Tables 1-2 (e.g. ~22% unaccounted in Table 1 entry 6) is also consistent with some undetected/loss material, mildly tensioning the 'no humins' claim. T4 strength/scope judgment routed to advisory; cannot deterministically confirm 'homogeneous throughout the entire coordinate' from the main text.

claims.text: The two reported side-product structures (IntA mixed acetal, IntB difurfuryl ether) are chemically plausible condensation products of the proposed intermediates (furfural + furfuryl alcohol + iPrOH; two furfuryl alcohols), with correct condensation stoichiometry (loss of one H2O each).
claims.quote: the 2-((furan-2-yl(isopropoxy)methoxy)methyl)furan, which was formed from FF via acetal formation ... derived from two molecules of FA
claims.predicate: Mass-balance/atom-balance of the proposed condensations: IntA from FF (C5H4O2) + furfuryl alcohol (C5H6O2) + iPrOH (C3H8O) - H2O should give C13H16O4 (acetal). IntB from 2x C5H6O2 - H2O = C10H10O3. Check atom balance closes.

evidence.quote: Model reaction 1 -> IntA + H2O ; Model reaction 2 -> IntB + H2O

verification.verifier_needed: Atom/mass-balance closure of the two condensation reactions (reactant atoms - H2O = product atoms).
verification.rationale: IntB: 2 x C5H6O2 (furfuryl alcohol) = C10H12O4; minus H2O = C10H10O3 (178.19), balances and matches the named ether. IntA: the mixed acetal C13H16O4 (236.27) is consistent with FF-derived acetal incorporating one iPrO and one furfuryloxy group with loss of water. Both Figure 6 model reactions correctly show '+ H2O' as the condensation byproduct. Atom balance closes. Confidence <1 because IntA's exact reactant set (which alcohols add) is inferred from the structure rather than an explicit balanced equation in the main text.

claims.text: Without acid (Ru-MACHO-BH alone, 30 min), furfural is converted 98% almost exclusively to furfuryl alcohol (FA 98%), establishing FA as the first intermediate (Table 1, entry 7).
claims.quote: Without acid, after 30 min reaction time [Ru-MACHO-BH] ... FA 98
claims.section: Table 1 entry 7 (footnote b: without acid, 30 min) + text
claims.predicate: Bind prose 'Ru-MACHO-BH alone gives FA' to Table 1 entry 7 (conv 98, FA 98) and check FA yield ~ conversion (selective hydrogenation to alcohol, no further products).

evidence.quote: 7 Ru-MACHO-BH(no acid) conv98 FA98

verification.verifier_needed: Prose-vs-table binding + yield~conversion sanity (FA 98 = conv 98).
verification.rationale: Table 1 entry 7 (no acid, 30 min) shows conv 98 / FA 98, i.e. essentially 100% selectivity to furfuryl alcohol, consistent with the mechanistic claim that the metal hydrogenates FF->FA first and the Bronsted acid is required for the downstream FA->FE->IPL steps. FA yield equals conversion (edge case, single product) - internally consistent. Clean prose-vs-table binding.

claims.text: The introduction's literature comparisons cite specific prior yields/conditions (e.g., de Vries Ru complex: 79% isolated IPL in iPrOH at 130 C; a Lu(OTf)3 catalyst: 53% IPL with humin formation; an Ir(III) half-sandwich complex A: FA in high yield at 85 C, 30 min, 0.5 mol%).

claims.text: Ru-MACHO-BH is the most active precatalyst for IPL formation among the screened Ru complexes, giving 50% IPL (with acid) vs <=15% for the dichloride complexes Ru-5 to Ru-8 (Table 1).
claims.quote: the most active Ru-complex in terms of isopropyl levulinate (IPL) formation ... was Ru-MACHO-BH

verification.verifier_needed: Argmax check over the Table 1 IPL column vs the 'most active for IPL' ranking claim.
verification.rationale: Ru-MACHO-BH gives the highest IPL yield in Table 1 (50%), exceeding Ru-MACHO (30%) and the dichlorides Ru-5..Ru-8 (3,4,6,15). The 'most active for IPL formation' ranking claim is consistent with the table argmax. Clean T2 max-of-column check.

claims.text: Both candidate intermediate-forming reactions (mixed-acetal IntA, dimer IntB) are acid-catalyzed, and the DFT comparison shows dimerization (IntB) is thermodynamically preferred over mixed-acetal formation (IntA) at both temperatures.
claims.quote: the formation of IntB is energetically more favorable than... IntA ... Both reactions are acid-catalyzed
claims.predicate: Comparative consistency: delta-G(IntB) < delta-G(IntA) at both 298 K (-14.9 < 26.1) and 403 K (-11.9 < 46.1); verify the 'IntB more favorable' claim follows from the Figure 6 numbers.

verification.verifier_needed: Pairwise comparison of the two free energies at each temperature vs the 'IntB more favorable' claim.
verification.rationale: At 298 K, IntB (-14.9) is 41.0 kJ/mol more favorable than IntA (+26.1); at 403 K, 58.0 kJ/mol more favorable (-11.9 vs +46.1). The comparative claim 'IntB energetically more favorable than IntA' follows directly from the Figure 6 values. Clean T2 numeric comparison; underlying DFT numbers not independently recomputed (SI Table S5).

claims.text: The paper reports the computational method as DLPNO-CCSD(T)/def2-TZVP // PBE0-D3/def2-TZVPP with SMD(2-propanol) solvation for the Figure 6 free-energy estimates.
claims.quote: Calculated at: DLPNO-CCSD(T)/def2-TZVP PBE0-D3/def2-TZVPP SMD = 2-propanol
claims.predicate: Method appropriateness: assess whether DLPNO-CCSD(T) single-points on PBE0-D3 geometries with SMD(2-propanol) is a reasonable level of theory for these small organic condensation free energies (yes); not a numeric recompute.

evidence.quote: DLPNO-CCSD(T)/def2-TZVP // PBE0-D3/def2-TZVPP, SMD = 2-propanol

verification.verifier_needed: Method-appropriateness review of the DFT/DLPNO protocol for small-molecule reaction free energies in solution.
verification.rationale: DLPNO-CCSD(T)/def2-TZVP single points on PBE0-D3/def2-TZVPP geometries with implicit SMD(2-propanol) solvation is a standard, defensible composite level for organic reaction thermochemistry; the solvent model matches the experimental solvent (iPrOH). This is a T3 appropriateness judgment, not deterministic; routed to advisory. Confidence reflects that the protocol is reasonable but the actual energies/thermal corrections live in SI Table S5 and are not recomputed.

claims.text: Product identification and yields throughout were determined by GC(-MS) using dodecane as an internal standard.
claims.quote: Product identification/conversion (%)/yield (%) were determined by GC(-MS) analysis using dodecane as an internal standard
claims.predicate: Method appropriateness: GC-MS with an internal standard (dodecane) is an appropriate quantification method for these volatile oxygenates; check the internal standard is suitable (inert, non-overlapping). Not a recompute.

evidence.quote: determined by GC(-MS) analysis using dodecane as an internal standard

verification.verifier_needed: Method-appropriateness check on GC-MS internal-standard quantification of the product set.
verification.rationale: GC(-MS) with dodecane as an inert internal standard is an appropriate and standard quantification method for these volatile furan-derived oxygenates (FF, FA, FE, IPL, AL, GVL); dodecane is non-reactive and typically well-resolved. T3 appropriateness judgment, routed to advisory. Confidence reflects that calibration/response-factor details would need the SI to fully validate quantification accuracy.

paper_level_notes: Real published ChemSusChem 2026 research article (DOI 10.1002/cssc.202502378, 19:e202502378), open access; authors Subramaniyan, Hering-Junghans, Barath (Leibniz-Institut fuer Katalyse / LIKAT Rostock). NOTE: the catalog filename says 'roy2025' but the actual authors are Subramaniyan/Hering-Junghans/Barath and publication year is 2026 - a corpus-id/metadata mismatch worth flagging. Deterministic frontier is rich: three yield/conversion tables (Tables 1-3) supporting clean T0 yield<=conversion and selectivity-bookkeeping checks; two molecular-weight assignments (236.27 -> C13H16O4 mixed acetal; 178.18 -> C10H10O3 difurfuryl ether) that recompute EXACTLY as T1 atomic-weight/atom-balance checks (both verified by hand here: 236.27 and 178.19); and concentration/mol% coherence checks (2 mmol/5 mL = 0.4 M; 1.5% MSA = 0.006 M; 0.5% KOH = 0.002 M; net acid 1.5-0.5 = 1.0 mol%) that all close. Several T2 prose-vs-table/figure bindings are checkable and mostly supported (15% GVL, 61(53)% IPL scale-up, Ru-MACHO-BH argmax for IPL, 285.2 mol% H3PO4 -> entry 7). The notable open item is the ABSTRACT's '67% IPL yield' which exceeds every IPL value printed in the main-text tables (max 61% GC / 59% MSA / 50% Table 1) and cannot be bound to a main-text figure/table - likely a peak from the Figure 5 time-course or SI Table S3/S4, flagged inconclusive (NOT manufactured as an error; needs SI). The word 'suppresses the formation of IPL with 59% yield' (c13) reads as a possible phrasing slip since 59% is the maximum IPL value. DFT free energies (Figure 6: IntA +26.1/+46.1; IntB -14.9/-11.9 kJ/mol) are sign/label/comparison-consistent with the prose but the values themselves require SI Table S5 (electronic energies + thermal corrections) to recompute - a T6/T2 boundary. SI is well-described: Supporting Tables S1-S5 (chemicals, dichloride-complex screening, exact overall-plot values S3, higher-FF-concentration S4, calculated DFT data S5) and Supporting Figures S1-S15 (NMR characterization, GC-MS chromatograms S9-S13, MS of the two side-products S14/S15). A formal Data Availability Statement is present ('data ... available in the supplementary material'). Mass balance never closes to 100% (unidentified/loss material, e.g. ~22% in Table 1 entry 6), which mildly tensions the 'no humins / homogeneous throughout' claim (c19, T4). No integrity-signal (T7) red flags observed in the rendered figures/tables. Overall this reads as a competent, internally consistent paper; the only genuine checkable tension is the 67% abstract IPL figure pending SI.
